life: S. Fulgentius was born in Africa of a noble family. From childhood he loved sacred learning and the service of God.
He became bishop amid persecution, and bore exile and hardship with a patient heart fixed upon Christ.
devotionSections: Each year may bring us fresh changes; faith teaches us to receive them from the hand of God.
"Lord, give me patience, and increase my faith."
An Arian priest betrayed Fulgentius, yet the saint answered injury with meekness and prayer.
"For patience is necessary for you, that, doing the will of God, you may receive the promise." Heb. x. 36.
source: Miniature Lives of the Saints, Vol. I

saint: The First Holy Martyrs of the Roman Church.
life: The first martyrs of Rome suffered under Nero after the burning of the city.
devotionSections: Let the remembrance of the martyrs strengthen us in lesser trials.
source: Miniature Lives of the Saints, Vol. I

life: Giovanni Colombini was a noble citizen of Siena who turned from worldly prosperity to the ardent love of God.
devotionSections: The love of God makes all burdens light and gives sweetness to every act of service.
source: Miniature Lives of the Saints, Vol. II

saint: S. Processus and S. Martinian, Martyrs.
life: Life section missing from the uploaded scan.
